title: spent 8 months building a model. my coworker beat it with two moving averages.
author: Important_Buy626
selftext:
1.8 sharpe on backtests. live for 6 weeks, underperforming expectations.

guy at work doesn't know what a sharpe ratio is. made more than my model this month. uses a 9 and 21 EMA cross.

8 months. 47 features. gradient boosting.

9 and 21 EMA.

genuinely asking how long do you run a live model before you decide the backtest was a lie?

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body: You need at least 50 trades, probably 3-6 months. And then 200 trades 6-12 months to say you have a good starting point.

replies.author: The_AI_Trader
replies.body:
But if you want to start trading your strategy , start with very small risk, and work your way up in small increments like 5-10% every 1-2 weeks. 

I would say do this after reaching a small subset group. 

One month is not indicative of anything. 

Best of luck! You got this!

comments.author: DreamfulTrader
comments.body:
It is because you think you are too intelligent and digging deeper and going a rabbit hole thinking with more rules and info it will be better. This is why most smart people in corporate where they are seen 'the smart people', fail miserably when they start their own business or start trading and blow loads of money and their saving while you see people or younger ones making profits. For trading and making money, you need to start simple, not overthinking, everyone is seeing the same chart.

\- If a simple strategy is not profitable, there is no need to go deeper. 8 months is too long to build a model especially with all the technology available, access to real time data and historical tick data to run a backtest.

\- if you cannot trade manually with the strategy or understand the logic what it is doing for entries and exits, then it is a waste trying to use AI, maching learning or algo trading.

\- backtesting on 2-3 years of data is more than enough. If it does not work, why bother with longer time period unless you are a fund manager or looking for generational wealth grow, life is short.

\- leave last 6 months to forward test your strategy. If good, then switch to live and let it run for 1-2 months. No need for longer.

\- understand how your backtest work v/s real time. If you don't understand how entries and exists are done in the backtest with your strategy, it will blow up when you run on real time - most people just run things without spending a few minutes to understand - this is one of your issue

\- if you strategy is not making at least $5-15 profit per contract per trade or per day profit (1% profit) , then you need to look into it

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
Hey guys I’ve my own ML trading system for BTC and Gold that I’ve been working on for a while.

It’s been backtested over 5 years and has averaged around 10% per month on fixed lot, with Sharpe above 3. And it's been love for around 8 months now.

I don’t currently have the capital to scale it myself, so I’m open to speaking with someone serious who may be interested in acquiring it.

If anyone interested to see the report and the live trades data just hit me up.

comments.author: antho727
comments.body:
You also need to make your backtesting engine so realistic that it basically mimic live trading. 
Also backtest with 5-10 years of data
